methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $3.5B.

data.narrative:
Perigee Aerospace is a Series D-stage space & aerospace company founded by Shin Dong-Yoon that has raised $56.64M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers in the same sector, with particular focus on 3 same-stage comparables: Astranis ($3.5B), Apex Space ($1.0B), and Stoke Space ($1.0B).

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis uses the $56.64M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D progression, yielding $85M. Peer-median valuation across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables produces $1.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple is not applicable because Perigee's revenue is not disclosed. The median of the peer set is $3.5B - $1.0B, with a median of $1.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $72M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $542M. This wide range reflects meaningful uncertainty around Perigee's position relative to peers—particularly whether the company tracks closer to early-stage capital deployment ($85M) or established Series D traction ($1.0B+). Key unknowns include revenue scale, customer concentration, and technical differentiation versus the comp set.
